structured.description: A module that provides an interface to the `readdir` system call using ctypes. It exposes the `readdir` function, which takes a directory path as input and returns a list of `dirent` objects representing the contents of the directory. The module uses the `ctypes` library to interact with the underlying system calls. Specifically, it defines classes for the `dirent` structure returned by `readdir`, and provides a high-level interface for working with directories using the `readdir` function.

items.description: Defines a structure to represent a directory entry, comprising various fields for storing information such as file type, name, and length.

attributes.name: _fields_
attributes.description: A list of field names and data types for the structure, including ino_t, off_t, d_reclen, d_type, and d_name.

items.name: _cdirent

items.description: Provides a way to represent directory entries as structures with various attributes such as type, name, and file status. It also allows for initialization with either a default value or an existing `cdirent` object.

attributes.name: DT_UNKNOWN
attributes.description: 0 or 1, representing the file type as unknown or not known, respectively.

attributes.name: DT_FIFO
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the file is a FIFO (first-in, first-out) stream.

attributes.name: DT_CHR
attributes.description: 0 or 1, indicating whether the directory entry is a character special device.

attributes.name: DT_DIR
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the file is a directory.

attributes.name: DT_BLK
attributes.description: 1 of the possible values for the `DT` field, indicating that the file is a block device.

attributes.name: DT_REG
attributes.description: 0 or 1 indicating if the directory entry is a regular file.

attributes.name: DT_LNK
attributes.description: Used to represent a symbolic link.

attributes.name: DT_WHT
attributes.description: 0 by default, indicating that the file is a whiteout.

items.name: dirent

items.description: Sets attributes of a `dirent` object based on those of an input `cdirent` object, either by copying values or setting them to `None`.

params.name: cdirent
params.description: Used to store an instance of the class `_CDirent`.

items.name: __init__

items.description: List directory entries. It opens a directory using the `_opendir` function, reads the contents of the directory using the `_readdir` function, and returns a list of directory entries using the `dirent` function.

params.name: directory
params.description: A path to a directory or file that represents the location from which entries will be read.

returns.description: A collection of directory entry objects.

items.name: readdir
